name: moat-open-questions-resolution
summary: Deep design session on content type distribution and provenance for syllago and MOAT. Resolved the skills frontmatter architecture, created a new canonical SKILL.md frontmatter spec, and updated the MOAT revised outline with OWASP AST10 findings. Three MOAT open questions remain unresolved (issues 3, 4, 6-8).
last_action: Created /home/hhewett/.local/src/moat/docs/skill-frontmatter-spec.md (new draft spec) and updated /home/hhewett/.local/src/moat/panel/moat-revised-outline.md with OWASP coverage map, scan_status, risk_tier, revocation feed, companion spec references, and per-item manifest entry definition.
next_action: Continue resolving open issues in /home/hhewett/.local/src/moat/panel/moat-revised-outline.md — specifically issues #3 (CRLF normalization, already resolved by linter/user but needs verification), #4 (registry manifest size and pagination), #6 (scan_status design decisions), #7 (risk_tier design decisions), and #8 (revocation/denouncement mechanism). Start by reading the full outline to get current state.
key_decisions: Skills frontmatter stays agentskills.io-compatible base + adds permissions + requires (from OWASP USF / agent ecosystem expectations). NO provenance fields in SKILL.md.

Provenance belongs at registry level (MOAT), not individual files. Evidence: circular hash problem, corpus data (2.8% failure rate on required fields), ClawHub poisoning, every production package ecosystem uses registry-level trust.
platforms field dropped — brittle (platform names are ephemeral), undermined by syllago's conversion capability.
.syllago.yaml is syllago implementation detail (not spec concern). Needs source_uri field added.
MOAT open issues 6 (scan_status), 7 (risk_tier), 8 (revocation) added to outline — all unresolved.

files_modified.changes: Added open issues 6-8 (scan_status, risk_tier, revocation), OWASP AST10 coverage map, scope boundary companion spec references, per-item manifest entry field list, revocation feed to normative requirements, 'Not a content format spec' to What This Is Not section

Created new draft spec — canonical SKILL.md frontmatter schema, implementation-agnostic, with full rationale for all decisions and evidence base for keeping provenance out of frontmatter. Includes References section with verified OWASP and Snyk URLs.

open_issues_in_moat_outline.3: CRLF normalization — marked RESOLVED by user/linter update but verify the resolution is complete
open_issues_in_moat_outline.4: Registry manifest size and pagination — unresolved
open_issues_in_moat_outline.6: scan_status in registry manifest entries — design questions: required vs optional, scanner name format, staleness threshold
open_issues_in_moat_outline.7: risk_tier in registry manifest entries — design questions: registry-assigned vs self-declared, normative vs advisory, tier boundary definitions per content type
open_issues_in_moat_outline.8: Revocation/denouncement mechanism — design questions: manifest section vs separate feed URL, client behavior on already-installed denounced content, cross-registry propagation
